text: Databases help us store and organize information efficiently. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases use tables with rows and columns, much like spreadsheets, making them great for structured data. To retrieve specific information, you write queries using the SQL language. Data modeling is the process of designing how tables relate to each other before building the database. Indexes speed up queries by allowing the database to find data without scanning every row. NoSQL databases store data differently, using documents or graphs, which makes them more flexible for unstructured or rapidly changing data. Choosing between SQL and NoSQL depends on your project needs.
